message:
fix(runtime): clamp LeakEscalation threshold=0 to 1 at construction and normalization

A zero leak-escalation threshold would trigger escalation (Panic or
Recover) on every obligation check, which is never the intended behavior
— it means "escalate after N leaked obligations" and N=0 is nonsensical.

Two defense layers:
1. LeakEscalation::new() — const-time clamping at construction so the
   invariant is established even outside RuntimeConfig::normalize().
2. RuntimeConfig::normalize() — runtime belt-and-suspenders check for
   configs deserialized from external sources that bypass the constructor.

Added two tests:
- test_leak_escalation_new_clamps_zero_threshold: verifies constructor
  clamps 0→1 while preserving the escalation policy (Panic)
- test_normalize_clamps_zero_leak_escalation_threshold: verifies
  normalize() clamps 0→1 on an existing Some(LeakEscalation) while
  preserving the Recover policy

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
